evals.description: Triggers on CLI argument parsing question
evals.prompt: How should I parse command-line arguments in my Node.js CLI tool?
How do I distribute my CLI tool so users can install it globally with npm?
I want to add an interactive setup wizard to my CLI. What library should I use?

evals.description: Can write a Commander.js CLI with subcommands and options
evals.prompt: Write a Node.js CLI using Commander.js with a 'build' subcommand that accepts --output and --watch flags.

evals.description: Can write a Python CLI with click decorators
evals.prompt: Write a Python CLI using click with a 'deploy' command that takes an environment argument and a --dry-run flag.

evals.description: Can implement config file loading with proper merge hierarchy
evals.prompt: Write a config loader for my Node.js CLI that reads from a dotfile, environment variables, and CLI flags with proper precedence.
Write a Node.js function that reads from a file argument or falls back to stdin when piped.

evals.description: Can produce correct exit codes for error scenarios
evals.prompt: What exit code should my CLI return when the user passes an invalid flag?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against printing errors to stdout
evals.prompt: In my CLI I use console.log to print error messages. Is that okay?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against requiring interactivity without fallback
evals.prompt: My CLI always asks for confirmation before deploying. Is there anything wrong with that?

evals.description: Gotcha: recommends kebab-case flags over camelCase
evals.prompt: Should I name my CLI flag --dryRun or --dry-run?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not invent non-standard flag conventions
evals.prompt: Should I use /verbose or +v as a flag in my CLI tool?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend argparse for complex Node.js CLIs
evals.prompt: What is the best argument parser for building a complex Node.js CLI with subcommands?

evals.description: References load: skill references argument-parsing-patterns file
evals.prompt: Where can I find detailed patterns for mutually exclusive flags and variadic arguments across multiple languages?
